07_ALT_status_tf_rate

Andrea Polazzi

2026-03-18

Setup

library(readxl)
source('../scripts/clean_columns.R')
data <- read_xlsx('../data/raw/PCAWG_variables_PCA_woK.xlsx')
## Warning: Expecting numeric in I1255 / R1255C9: got 'NA'
## Warning: Expecting numeric in I1724 / R1724C9: got 'NA'
clean_data <- clean_pcawg_data(data)
model_data_reduced <- clean_data[complete.cases(clean_data), -c(1,2,4)]  # we remove also tf_rate
summary(model_data_reduced)
##     alt_status  telomere_insertion_rate telomere_content_log2
##  ALT-low :768   Min.   :0.00000         Min.   :-2.29092     
##  ALT-high: 49   1st Qu.:0.00000         1st Qu.:-0.95324     
##                 Median :0.00000         Median :-0.55712

##                 3rd Qu.:0.01934         3rd Qu.:-0.08914

##  1st Qu.: 0.02014   1st Qu.:-0.56491      1st Qu.:-0.2686      
##  Median : 0.10907   Median : 0.02487      Median : 0.1495      
##  Mean   : 0.91717   Mean   : 0.01321      Mean   : 0.1797      
##  3rd Qu.: 0.39077   3rd Qu.: 0.59111      3rd Qu.: 0.6213      
##  Max.   :53.76790   Max.   : 4.47470      Max.   : 2.8541

##  Min.   :-2.43840      Min.   :-3.13241      Min.   :-2.8553      
##  1st Qu.:-0.32314      1st Qu.:-0.53764      1st Qu.:-0.4778

PCA without TF rate

X <- scale(model_data_reduced[, -1])
pca <- prcomp(X, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
summary(pca)
## Importance of components:
##                           PC1    PC2    PC3     PC4    PC5     PC6     PC7
## Standard deviation     1.7177 1.2707 1.1362 0.97558 0.9308 0.88213 0.85099
## Proportion of Variance 0.2459 0.1346 0.1076 0.07931 0.0722 0.06485 0.06035
## Cumulative Proportion  0.2459 0.3804 0.4880 0.56732 0.6395 0.70436 0.76471
##                            PC8     PC9    PC10    PC11    PC12
## Standard deviation     0.81344 0.79393 0.73327 0.72694 0.68215
## Proportion of Variance 0.05514 0.05253 0.04481 0.04404 0.03878
## Cumulative Proportion  0.81985 0.87238 0.91719 0.96122 1.00000
library(ggplot2)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2
pca_df <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca$x[,1],
  PC2 = pca$x[,2],
  ALT = model_data_reduced$alt_status
)

ggplot(pca_df, aes(PC1, PC2, color = ALT)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA without TF rate",
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2"
  )

Biplot

# sample coordinates
scores <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca$x[,1],
  PC2 = pca$x[,2],
  ALT = model_data_reduced$alt_status
)

# motif vectors
loadings <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca$rotation[,1],
  PC2 = pca$rotation[,2],
  comp_names = c(rownames(pca$rotation)[1:3], sapply(strsplit(rownames(pca$rotation)[4:12], split = '_', fixed = TRUE), `[`, 1))
)

# scale the loadings (smaller than sample coordinates) to make arrows more visible
loadings$PC1 <- loadings$PC1 * 10
loadings$PC2 <- loadings$PC2 * 10

library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
## Warning: package 'ggrepel' was built under R version 4.5.2
p <- ggplot(scores, aes(PC1, PC2, color = ALT)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +

  geom_segment(data = loadings,
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = PC1, yend = PC2),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25,"cm")),
               inherit.aes = FALSE,
               color = "black") +

  geom_text_repel(data = loadings,
                  aes(PC1, PC2, label = comp_names),
                  inherit.aes = FALSE,
                  size = 3.5) +

  theme_bw() +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA biplot without TF rate",
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2"
  )

plot(p)

ggsave("../outputs/PCA_biplot_red_tf.pdf", plot = p, width = 7, height = 6)

Split the dataset

set.seed(21)

train_index <- sample(1:nrow(model_data_reduced), size=0.7*nrow(model_data_reduced))
train_red <- model_data_reduced[train_index,]
test_red <- model_data_reduced[-train_index,]

# Check distribution
table(train_red$alt_status, dnn = 'train')
## train
##  ALT-low ALT-high 
##      537       34
table(test_red$alt_status, dnn = 'test')
## test
##  ALT-low ALT-high 
##      231       15

First classifier: Logistic Regression

logreg_model_reduced <- glm(
  alt_status ~ .,
  data = train_red,
  family = "binomial"
)
## Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred
prob_red <- predict(
  logreg_model_reduced,
  test_red,
  type = "response"
)

Evaluate performance

library(pROC, quietly = T)
## Type 'citation("pROC")' for a citation.
## 
## Attaching package: 'pROC'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     cov, smooth, var
roc_red <- roc(
  test_red$alt_status,
  prob_red,
  levels = c("ALT-low","ALT-high")
)
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_red, main = 'ROC curve - Log. Reg. (all features except TF rate)')

auc(roc_red)
## Area under the curve: 0.9983
prob_df <- data.frame(
  prob = prob_red,
  alt_status = test_red$alt_status
)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(prob_df, aes(x = prob, fill = alt_status)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted probability distributions",
    x = "Predicted probability of ALT-high",
    y = "Density"
  )

Other metrics

pred_class <- ifelse(prob_red >= 0.5, "ALT-high", "ALT-low")
pred_class <- factor(pred_class, levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high"))

library(caret, quietly = TRUE)
## Warning: package 'lattice' was built under R version 4.5.2
confusionMatrix(pred_class, test_red$alt_status, positive = 'ALT-high')
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction ALT-low ALT-high
##   ALT-low      229        0
##   ALT-high       2       15
##                                          
##                Accuracy : 0.9919         
##                  95% CI : (0.9709, 0.999)
##     No Information Rate : 0.939          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 2.735e-05      
##                                          
##                   Kappa : 0.9332         
##                                          
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.4795         
##                                          
##             Sensitivity : 1.00000        
##             Specificity : 0.99134        
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.88235        
##          Neg Pred Value : 1.00000        
##              Prevalence : 0.06098        
##          Detection Rate : 0.06098        
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.06911        
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.99567        
##                                          
##        'Positive' Class : ALT-high       
##

Second classifier: Random Forest

library(randomForest, quietly = T)
## randomForest 4.7-1.2
## Type rfNews() to see new features/changes/bug fixes.
## 
## Attaching package: 'randomForest'
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     margin
rf_red <- randomForest(
  alt_status ~ .,
  data = train_red,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE
)

rf_pred_red <- predict(
  rf_red,
  test_red,
  type = "prob"
)[,"ALT-high"]

Evaluate performance

roc_rf_red <- roc(
  test_red$alt_status,
  rf_pred_red,
  levels = c("ALT-low","ALT-high")
)
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_rf_red, main = 'ROC curve - RF (all features except TF rate)')

auc(roc_rf_red)
## Area under the curve: 0.9867

Other metrics

rf_pred_red <- ifelse(rf_pred_red >= 0.5, "ALT-high", "ALT-low")
rf_pred_red <- factor(rf_pred_red, levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high"))

library(caret)

confusionMatrix(
  rf_pred_red,
  test_red$alt_status,
  positive = "ALT-high"
)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction ALT-low ALT-high
##   ALT-low      230        6
##   ALT-high       1        9
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.9715          
##                  95% CI : (0.9423, 0.9885)
##     No Information Rate : 0.939           
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.01553         
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.7056          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.13057         
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.60000         
##             Specificity : 0.99567         
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.90000         
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.97458         
##              Prevalence : 0.06098         
##          Detection Rate : 0.03659         
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.04065         
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.79784         
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : ALT-high        
## 
varImpPlot(rf_red, main = 'Variable importance plot - Random Forest (all features except TF rate)' )

Cross Validation of the performances

To assess the robustness of these two predictive models, we conducted repeated stratified cross-validation.

Logistic Regression - CV

library(pROC)

set.seed(21)

cv_data <- model_data_reduced
k <- 5
n_repeats <- 5

auc_results <- c()

for (r in 1:n_repeats) {
  # stratified fold assignment
  fold_id <- integer(nrow(cv_data))
  
  idx_low  <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-low")
  idx_high <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-high")
  
  fold_id[idx_low]  <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_low)))
  fold_id[idx_high] <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_high)))
  
  for (fold in 1:k) {
    train_cv <- cv_data[fold_id != fold, ]
    test_cv  <- cv_data[fold_id == fold, ]
    
    logreg_cv_model <- glm(alt_status~., data = train_cv, family = 'binomial')
    
    logreg_cv_prob <- predict(logreg_cv_model, test_cv, type = 'response')
    
    roc_obj <- roc(
      response = test_cv$alt_status,
      predictor = logreg_cv_prob,
      levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high")
    )
    
    auc_results <- c(auc_results, as.numeric(auc(roc_obj)))
  }
}

mean(auc_results)
## [1] 0.9613965
# sd(auc_results)
# auc_results

Random Forest - CV

library(randomForest)
library(pROC)

set.seed(21)

cv_data <- model_data_reduced
k <- 5
n_repeats <- 5

auc_results <- c()

for (r in 1:n_repeats) {
  # stratified fold assignment
  fold_id <- integer(nrow(cv_data))
  
  idx_low  <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-low")
  idx_high <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-high")
  
  fold_id[idx_low]  <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_low)))
  fold_id[idx_high] <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_high)))
  
  for (fold in 1:k) {
    train_cv <- cv_data[fold_id != fold, ]
    test_cv  <- cv_data[fold_id == fold, ]
    
    rf_cv_model <- randomForest(
      alt_status ~ .,
      data = train_cv,
      ntree = 500,
      mtry = 2
    )
    
    rf_cv_prob <- predict(rf_cv_model, test_cv, type = "prob")[, "ALT-high"]
    
    roc_obj <- roc(
      response = test_cv$alt_status,
      predictor = rf_cv_prob,
      levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high")
    )
    
    auc_results <- c(auc_results, as.numeric(auc(roc_obj)))
  }
}

mean(auc_results)
## [1] 0.9828611
# sd(auc_results)
# auc_results

These results confirm the robustness of the AUC values previously recorded for the two models, and agree with the fact that allowing non-linear relationships among features (i.e. using Random Forest) provide a better classification power.
